# BlocLabs Developer Onboarding Program

> From junior developer to AI-augmented senior engineer. Language-agnostic foundations, mental models, engineering craft, and AI-augmented development. 6 phases, 44 guides covering foundation through mastery.

## Phase 1: Foundation

Get your machine ready and learn the tools of the trade.

- [macOS Setup](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-1-·-macOS-Setup): System config, Xcode CLI tools, essential settings
- [Terminal & Shell](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-1-·-Terminal-and-Shell): Zsh, Oh My Zsh, navigation, piping, scripting basics
- [Homebrew](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-1-·-Homebrew): Package management on macOS
- [Git Fundamentals](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-1-·-Git-Fundamentals): Mental model for version control, branching, PRs
- [Dev Tools](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-1-·-Dev-Tools): Node, Go, Python, Rust, Docker installation and verification
- [Editor Setup](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-1-·-Editor-Setup): Cursor, VS Code, extensions, keybindings, productivity

## Phase 2: Mental Models

Understand the machine and the principles. Language-agnostic, career-defining.

- [How Computers Work](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-2-·-How-Computers-Work): CPU, memory hierarchy, disk, I/O — what's under your code
- [Data & Algorithms](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-2-·-Data-and-Algorithms): Structures, complexity, trade-offs — think before you code
- [Systems Thinking](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-2-·-Systems-Thinking): Inputs/outputs, feedback loops, emergence, second-order effects
- [Networking Fundamentals](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-2-·-Networking-Fundamentals): TCP/IP, HTTP, DNS, TLS, WebSockets — how the internet works
- [Databases & State](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-2-·-Databases-and-State): SQL, ACID, CAP theorem, caching — where data lives
- [Problem Solving](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-2-·-Problem-Solving): Debugging as discipline, scientific method for code
- [Operating Systems](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-2-·-Operating-Systems): Processes, threads, memory management, filesystems

## Phase 3: Engineering Craft

Write code that lasts. The standards that separate professionals from hobbyists.

- [Code as Craft](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-3-·-Code-as-Craft): Readability, naming, correctness > cleverness
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-3-·-Error-Handling): The main code path, not an afterthought
- [Testing](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-3-·-Testing): Pyramid, what to test, TDD when it fits
- [Architecture](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-3-·-Architecture): Layering, dependency rules, clean boundaries
- [API Design](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-3-·-API-Design): REST, contracts, versioning, backward compatibility
- [Observability](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-3-·-Observability): Logs, metrics, traces — if you can't see it, you can't fix it
- [Reading Code](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-3-·-Reading-Code): Navigate codebases, trace data flow, review PRs
- [Developer Communication](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-3-·-Developer-Communication): PRs, commits, docs, asking for help, incident reports

## Phase 4: AI-Augmented Development

The force multiplier. How we work at BlocLabs.

- [AI Tools Overview](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-4-·-AI-Tools-Overview): Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, OpenClaw
- [Models & When to Use Them](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-4-·-Models-and-When-to-Use-Them): Opus vs Sonnet vs GPT vs Gemini — trade-offs
- [Prompt Engineering](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-4-·-Prompt-Engineering): How to talk to AI effectively for code
- [Code Review with AI](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-4-·-Code-Review-with-AI): Using AI to review, not replace judgment
- [AI Ethics & Limits](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-4-·-AI-Ethics-and-Limits): What AI gets wrong, verification, IP, licensing

## Phase 5: BlocLabs Stack

Our tools, our patterns, our workflow.

- [Go at BlocLabs](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-5-·-Go-at-BlocLabs): Our Go patterns and conventions
- [Rust at BlocLabs](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-5-·-Rust-at-BlocLabs): When and why we use Rust
- [JavaScript & TypeScript](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-5-·-JavaScript-and-TypeScript): Frontend, Node.js, TypeScript patterns
- [Python for Developers](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-5-·-Python-for-Developers): Scripting, data processing, ML pipelines
- [PostgreSQL](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-5-·-PostgreSQL): Schema design, migrations, query performance
- [AWS & Cloudflare](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-5-·-AWS-and-Cloudflare): Services we use, when, why
- [Docker & Containers](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-5-·-Docker-and-Containers): Containerization, compose, registry
- [Development Workflow](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-5-·-Development-Workflow): Linear + GitHub, branch naming, PR process

## Phase 6: Mastery

The path to staff engineer and beyond.

- [System Design](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-6-·-System-Design): Distributed systems, scaling, trade-offs
- [Domain-Driven Design](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-6-·-Domain-Driven-Design): Bounded contexts, aggregates, event sourcing
- [Performance](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-6-·-Performance): Profiling, bottlenecks, optimization
- [Security](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Phase-6-·-Security): OWASP, auth, secrets management, threat modeling

## Guides

Practical reference guides for daily engineering work.

- [10x Engineer with Claude Code](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Guide-·-10x-Engineer-with-Claude-Code): Practical guide for maximizing productivity with Claude Code and BlocLabs engineering standards
- [Claude Code Weekly Tips](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Guide-·-Claude-Code-Weekly-Tips): 12-week mastery program, one tip per week

## Supplementary Tracks

Optional deep-dive tracks for specialized roles.

- [Apple / iOS Track](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Supplementary-·-Apple-iOS-Track): Swift, SwiftUI, mobile development
- [Blockchain Track](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Supplementary-·-Blockchain-Track): EVM, Solana, smart contracts
- [Infrastructure Track](https://github.com/BloclabsHQ/developer-onboarding/wiki/Supplementary-·-Infrastructure-Track): Deep AWS, Terraform, monitoring
